extension.description: PureBasic language support for Visual Studio Code with syntax highlighting, language server, and development tools
theme.constantsHighlight.label: PureBasic (Constants Highlight)
config.purebasic.maxNumberOfProblems.des: Maximum number of problems (diagnostics) reported per file.
Path to PureBasic Compilers/APIFunctionListing.txt to enable OS API IntelliSense (native API calls with "_" suffix).
config.purebasic.completion.autoClosingP: Enable auto-closing of brackets and quotes.
config.purebasic.linting.checkUnusedVari: Check for unused variables and procedures.
config.purebasic.linting.checkUndefinedS: Check for undefined symbols and references.
config.purebasic.linting.enableCodeActio: Enable code actions and quick fixes.
config.purebasic.symbols.cacheEnabled.de: Enable symbol caching for better performance.
config.purebasic.symbols.cacheSize.descr: Maximum number of symbols to cache.
config.purebasic.performance.enableIncre: Enable incremental parsing for better performance with large files.
config.purebasic.performance.maxFileSize: Maximum file size in bytes for processing (default: 1MB).
config.purebasic.build.compiler.descript: Path to the PureBasic command line compiler (pbcompiler). Leave empty to use 'pbcompiler' from PATH.
config.purebasic.build.fallbackSource.de: Build context source when no .pbp project is active (No Project mode or pb-project-files not installed).
config.purebasic.build.fallbackSource.so: Read build settings from the IDE metadata block at the end of the current source file ('; IDE Options = ...').
config.purebasic.build.fallbackSource.la: Read build settings from .vscode/launch.json (purebasic configuration entry).
config.purebasic.build.fallbackSource.fi: Read build settings from a sidecar file named '<filename>.pb.cfg' next to the source file.
config.purebasic.build.fallbackSource.pr: Read build settings from a 'project.cfg' file, searching upward from the source file's directory.
config.purebasic.run.mode.description: How to run the compiled PureBasic executable.
config.purebasic.run.mode.spawn.descript: Spawn a child process; output is streamed to the Output channel (shell-safe).
config.purebasic.run.mode.terminal.descr: Open an integrated terminal; supports interactive programs and ANSI colors.
command.showDiagnostics.title: PureBasic: Show Diagnostics
command.restartLanguageServer.title: PureBasic: Restart Language Server
command.clearSymbolCache.title: PureBasic: Clear Symbol Cache
command.formatDocument.title: PureBasic: Format Document
command.findSymbols.title: PureBasic: Find Symbols in Workspace
command.buildActiveTarget.title: PureBasic: Build Active Target
command.runActiveTarget.title: PureBasic: Run Active Target
command.buildAndRunActiveTarget.title: PureBasic: Build & Run Active Target
debugger.launch.program.description: Absolute path to the PureBasic executable or compiled binary.
debugger.launch.compiler.description: Path to pbcompiler executable (platform-specific; default: 'pbcompiler' on PATH).
debugger.launch.transport.description: Debugger transport mode. auto = Windows uses pipe, Linux/macOS uses fifo. fifo = named pipes (Linux/macOS only).
debugger.launch.debugHost.description: Host used for network debugger transport.
debugger.launch.debugPort.description: Port used for network debugger transport (0 = random free port).
debugger.launch.debugPassword.descriptio: Optional password for PureBasic network debugger transport.
debugger.launch.stopOnEntry.description: Automatically stop at the first line of the program.
debugger.launch.trace.description: Enable verbose adapter trace logging (written to stderr).
debugger.launch.compilerArgs.description: Full argument list passed to pbcompiler (including the source file). Usually provided automatically from the active .pbp target.
debugger.launch.cwd.description: Working directory used for the pbcompiler invocation.
debugger.launch.output.description: Expected output file created by pbcompiler for this debug session (usually a temporary file).
debugger.launch.runCwd.description: Working directory used when launching the debuggee executable.
debugger.launch.runArgs.description: Command line arguments passed to the debuggee executable.
